title: Cyananthus pilosus

Cyananthus pilosus is a member of the genus Cyananthus, which comprises several species of alpine plants native to the Himalayan region. The plant's classification places it within the order Asterales, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. intro: Cyananthus pilosus, also known as the hairy cyananthus or bluebells of Tibet, is a flowering plant species native to the Himalayas and the Tibetan Plateau. The plant has been traditionally used in Tibetan medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Cyananthus pilosus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or a tincture, and it is often combined with other herbs to create a medicinal preparation that is used to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
